title: No Access Control on setProtocolFee
reason: Rejected: `setProtocolFee()` is protected by `requiresAuth`, so arbitrary callers cannot change it.

title: Unprotected claimFees allows stealing accumulated fees
reason: Rejected: `claimFees()` only pays out `rewards[msg.sender][token]`; callers cannot withdraw another account's rewards.

title: No minimum protocol fee enforcement
reason: Rejected: this is a documentation/governance mismatch, not a realistic exploit path causing protocol-level harm.

title: Fee-on-transfer token handling breaks for legitimate tokens
reason: Rejected: fee-on-transfer tokens are explicitly treated as unsupported and the code reverts rather than silently mis-accounting or losing funds.

title: Missing access control on setReferrerFee
reason: Rejected: letting each referrer set its own fee is the intended design; it does not let third parties alter someone else's fee setting.

title: Unchecked callback return values
reason: Rejected: the teller verifies that its payout-token balance increased by at least `payout_`, so the callback must actually fund the full payout amount or the transaction reverts.

title: ERC20BondToken Mint/Burn Access Control
reason: Rejected: cloned bond tokens embed the teller address in immutable args, and the implementation contract itself does not become freely mintable by arbitrary users.

title: Guardian parameter unused in Auth contract
reason: Rejected: `guardian_` is passed into `Auth` as the owner parameter, so it is not unused; this is naming confusion, not a vulnerability.
